name: Acute Gout Diagnosis Rule
description: Risk stratifies for gout vs non-gout arthritis and helps determine which patients benefit most from joint aspiration.
purpose: Diagnosis of gout in patients with new monoarthritis
when_to_use: A patient with a new monoarthritis

items.label: cvd
items.description: Hypertension or ≥1 cardiac diseases (Angina, MI, CHF, Stroke/TIA, PVD)

formula: male + attack + onset + red + mtp + cvd + uric

interpretation.description: Unlikely gout (prevalence of gout 2.2%). Other causes of monoarthritis should be considered, for example: pseudogout, septic arthritis, reactive arthritis, psoriatic, rheumatoid, or osteoarthritis.
Indeterminate (prevalence of gout 31.2%). These patients are most likely to benefit from joint aspiration and fluid analysis for urate crystals.
Gout very likely (prevalence of gout 80.4%). Empiric gout medications can be started as opposed to more generic arthritis treatments.

references.citation: Janssens HJ, Fransen J, van de Lisdonk EH, van Riel PL, van Weel C, Janssen M. A diagnostic rule for acute gouty arthritis in primary care without joint fluid analysis. Arch Intern Med. 2010 Jul 12;170(13):1120-6.

notes: Originally studied and validated in two primary care cohorts in Europe, so may not be as applicable in non-European populations. AUC for the receiver operator curves from the original and validation studies ranged from 0.86-0.89. The rule improves on clinician gestalt: PPV of the clinical diagnosis may improve from 0.64 to 0.87 and the NPV from 0.87 to 0.95. Even the 'gold standard' of monosodium urate crystals in fluid analysis is falsely negative about 5% of the time.
